paragraphs.text: Hello. Would it be possible to expand the scope of this legislation to include something about purchased media like video games and maybe others like movies? In other words I see it as important if the topic of the Stop Killing Games movement and the Stop Destroying Videogames EU citizens initiative would be looked at. Also some actions of two major credit card companies regarding their demands on sites that you can buy games from, should maybe be looked at too since it affects for example the freedom of both companies and consumers since there isn't really any other good enough alternatives to these two credit card companies' payment methods resulting in completely legal media becoming unavailable due to these credit card companies not liking them and many platforms needing the payment method options for their services. It's also pretty possible there will be more meddling by these two companies in the future since this attempt worked so how much do they want to actually dictate what consumers buy and companies can publish? Things would probably be different if there were more of these credit card companies (on the same level as these two) so consumers and companies would have the option to then use the other ones and the current two can then dictate the use of their services without affecting the freedoms of others as much. I would like to write more and better examples and to just clarify stuff better but I'm not good at writing and there's so much well written feedback done already regarding these issues. I just want to show how I see these things as important and worth looking into and considering. Thank you for your important work and time. I hope you have a nice day.
